text_blocks.snippet: In Vancouver, numerous resources are available to help manage stress, ranging from immediate crisis support to long-term counselling and self-help programs.
If you are in immediate danger or distress:

list.snippet: Emergency: Call 9-1-1 or visit the nearest hospital emergency department.
Crisis Line: Call 9-8-8 (National Suicide Crisis Helpline) or 1-800-SUICIDE (1-800-784-2433).
Mental Health Support Line: Call 310-6789 (no area code needed) for 24/7 emotional support and information.
Vancouver Access and Assessment Centre (AAC): Provides on-site assessment and crisis intervention, (604) 675-3700 (7:30 a.m. to 10:00 p.m.).
Canadian Mental Health Association (CMHA) - North and West Vancouver: Offers short-term, low-cost counseling (604) 987-6959.
Family Services of the North Shore: Provides free counselling support for up to 6 weeks.
REACH Medical Clinic: Offers free virtual counselling sessions covered by MSP.
Moving Forward Family Services: Provides affordable, virtual counselling.
Wait-list Services: If you need to speak to someone immediately, call a crisis line first.
BounceBack®: A free, evidence-based program offering online/phone-based coaching for adults and youth (13+) with stress, anxiety, or low mood.
Living Life to the Full: An 8-session course teaching practical skills for dealing with life's challenges.
MindShift™ CBT App: A free app based on Cognitive Behavioural Therapy to help manage anxiety.
Anxiety Canada: Provides free, science-based resources and self-management guides.
Indigenous Peoples: KUU-US Crisis Response Service (1-800-588-8717) and Hope for Wellness (1-855-242-3310).
Youth & Young Adults (12-24): Foundry Virtual BC offers free counseling, peer support, and group sessions.
Seniors: Seniors Distress Line (604-872-1234) and Seniors Abuse & Information Line (1-866-437-1940).
LGBTQ2S+: Qmunity provides free counselling and support groups.
Healthcare Workers: Care to Speak (1-866-802-7337) offers peer support.
Group Drumming: Offered at Champlain Heights Community Centre.
Sound Bath & Meditation: Available at Mount Seymour United Church and Saltwonder Himalayan Salt Cave.
Yoga: Winter Yoga and Tea events are available, such as those at Dr. Sun Yat-Sen Classical Chinese Garden.

text_blocks.snippet: For more, you can call 8-1-1 at any time to speak with a nurse for guidance on health services and resources in your area.
